name: Liz Cheney
subject: Zoe Lofgren is an unsung hero
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LOFGREN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | | Dear Peter,

My friend Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren is an unsung hero for defending our democracy and our Constitution.

I had not known Zoe well before our work together on the House Select Committee to investigate the January 6th attack on the United States Capitol, but I was aware of her reputation as one of the most effective and respected Members of the House of Representatives. She is a thoughtful and serious member, and she operates with zero ego — a rarity in Congress.

Zoe has been a source of wisdom and good judgment for me and many Members in Congress. Although we don’t share many views on politics or public policy, I can assure you that we need Zoe in Congress and on the frontlines as we continue to protect and secure the future of our democracy.

We all must look beyond party and support people like Zoe who will defend the Constitution, someone we know will do the right thing. _We need good, smart, honest and honorable people in elected office._

[**_With your help we can make sure Zoe has the resources needed to stay in Congress and continue the important work of defending our Constitution. Please join me in supporting her and contributing $10 today._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109652128/551794205/-1469323401?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-06-15_LIZ_ZOL_ES-25464_RS&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NzllNWZlMC00NDRhLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hX3YVIsOPOd-08HcgiS--psji6eWdClJR5lNWbwgpo4=)

I hope I can count on you to join me. [**_We need to support and stand with Zoe now!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109652129/551794206/-1469323401?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-06-15_LIZ_ZOL_ES-25464_RS&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NzllNWZlMC00NDRhLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hX3YVIsOPOd-08HcgiS--psji6eWdClJR5lNWbwgpo4=)

Best,

Liz Cheney

subject: There’s More Work Ahead — Join Me in What’s Next
disclaimer_text: Paid for by David Canepa for Supervisor 2024, ID# 1399463**
body:
| | | | [ ](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=c29b130285&e=c0babbc643)

| | | Dear Friend, I hope this note finds you well, and that your summer is off to a great start! As I reflect on my work serving San Mateo County, I am grateful for your continued support in helping me achieve so much for our community. Together, we’ve worked to address critical priorities like public safety, housing affordability, the environment, enhancing children, and saving family services. ** [David Canepa — Donate via ActBlue](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=2f56911b70&e=c0babbc643)** While my current role has allowed me to make a meaningful impact, I know there is still more work to be done. As I look toward the future and the opportunities ahead, I would love to have your continued support. Your encouragement, ideas, and generosity have always been invaluable, and they will continue to help create a better future. ** [David Canepa — Donate via ActBlue](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=40b3879682&e=c0babbc643)**

I am eager to hear what’s most important to you as we continue working to make San Mateo County a place we are all proud to call home.

In friendship,

David Canepa
Board President, San Mateo County
[](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=00e76efc87&e=c0babbc643) **Paid for by David Canepa for Supervisor 2024, ID# 1399463**

Peter, I am shocked. I have reached out to 50+ supporters today, and you are the first person who has taken the time to read my message.

I sincerely hope that you listen to what I need to say.

Now, since you are doing me a favor, I want to be completely honest with you..... However, I fear you might not like what I have to say.

I am Young Kim, an immigrant, a mother, a conservative American Patriot, and a chief irritant to career politicians in Washington.

Unfortunately, I do not fit in the category that many in Washington would like to place me in:

I am a woman, I own a small business, and I am **NOT** a part of the wealthy elite. I was not born as an American citizen, and English is not my first language. However, I am a proud American Patriot, tried and true.

Now, I am in Washington fighting for **YOU** and other everyday Americans who want to see lower taxes and the American Dream protected.

But, I am **REVOLTED** by what I've seen here, and you should be too.

Peter, so many in Washington don't care about you, but I do.

There are too many politicians who are there for themselves, furthering their own agendas and tossing the everyday American, like you and me, under the bus. They want **MORE** power, **MORE** influence, and **MORE** money - all for themselves. These lawmakers do not care about you, and it is **APPALLING**.

All I can say is: I call it like I see it, and I do not like what I've seen, but I will never let these career politicians forget about **YOU**. This is how I have found myself as a major target of Left-Wing Super PACs and Democrat campaign funds, who are desperately trying to defeat my battleground campaign in this cycle.

Again, to be forthcoming..... clean_body: I sincerely hope you listen to what I need to say. I sincerely hope you listen to what I need to say. | | | | | | Peter, I am shocked. I have reached out to 50+ supporters today, and you are the first person who has taken the time to read my message. I sincerely hope that you listen to what I need to say. Now, since you are doing me a favor, I want to be completely honest with you..... However, I fear you might not like what I have to say. I am Young Kim, an immigrant, a mother, a conservative American Patriot, and a chief irritant to career politicians in Washington. Unfortunately, I do not fit in the category that many in Washington would like to place me in: I am a woman, I own a small business, and I am NOT a part of the wealthy elite. I was not born as an American citizen, and English is not my first language. However, I am a proud American Patriot, tried and true. Now, I am in Washington fighting for YOU and other everyday Americans who want to see lower taxes and the American Dream protected. But, I am REVOLTED by what I've seen here, and you should be too. Peter, so many in Washington don't care about you, but I do. There are too many politicians who are there for themselves, furthering their own agendas and tossing the everyday American, like you and me, under the bus. They want MORE power, MORE influence, and MORE money - all for themselves. These lawmakers do not care about you, and it is APPALLING. All I can say is: I call it like I see it, and I do not like what I've seen, but I will never let these career politicians forget about YOU. This is how I have found myself as a major target of Left-Wing Super PACs and Democrat campaign funds, who are desperately trying to defeat my battleground campaign in this cycle. Again, to be forthcoming..... I fear this time they have a good chance to succeed if I do not meet my fundraising goals. | | | "So please, I humbly ask, will you be one of the FEW patriots to chip in today to keep this small business owning mother and immigrant fighting for YOU in Washington? Help me keep our majority in the House! " I will NEVER forget your generosity and support. | | - Young Kim (CA-40) | | | Paid for by Young Kim for Congress P.O. Box 17490 Anaheim, CA 92817 | |

It's Austin. Instead of helping our communities, Donald Trump and House Republicans are attempting to gut Medicaid and SNAP — stripping health care and food assistance from millions of families — just so billionaires can walk away with another tax break. And while they try to ram this bill through the Senate, Republicans are also working to hand Trump even more unchecked power — the ability to ignore the courts, defy contempt orders, and operate above the law. It’s a dangerous attempt to concentrate control in the hands of one man, no matter the cost. Southern New Mexico deserves leadership that will put its people before political gain, and Gabe Vasquez is that leader. Gabe will never stop fighting to make sure families can afford to see a doctor, kids have enough to eat, and rural clinics and schools have the resources they need to stay open. [We’re fighting back with everything we’ve got — but to keep up the pressure, we must hit our $6,000 mid-month fundraising goal by midnight tonight. I've been in some tough spots--combat zones, bootstrapping a business from scratch, and now this fight to protect **OUR** seat in Congress.

**The Democrats are desperate to flip this district. They've thrown over $100 million at places like ours because they know that if they take this seat, they take the House --and everything we believe in goes with it.**

_**I don't claim to be a career politician. I'm a soldier who knows what it means to fight for what matters. I'm a dad who fights for the future his kids deserve.**_

I'm running this race with my heart on the line, and I'm here to fight for you--the hardworking people who've been forgotten by Washington's elite.

But this fight is bigger than me. It's about keeping a conservative majority in the House, so we can protect our values, our freedoms, and our future.

**[I can't do this without you.](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=99a22f3f453f4c79a55626c1dc359e21&_e=jndkyAFdyAhVyo-8s3aWHd-9B2974FiaPf0R6CagLNZat-hEZueE0Csq7LH32HvBG6ZWnKuXUsy4WIgFz5dxL9086CU9GgfHkCGtPFYckpDP_TmslB0kao6vppedRF4hG6GGo7RsuW6qnzlarKXd5S6KchSPqZPUfZjCOsiaE6u1A-0_oiAzPzlmkoLQaXT3ixYm4MS_wIk3AsXSU5iPRj9uGOXVqQViXkRjWkFLuvMCaiUSLR9WuWt2s5cus4SWm8NdtH_yx3yg7T50Q_QiB-vSg8zqJV_qLf1F3YklXIZq8EOWhq_aSkA-dB3-_2aHWlFDt3r5o_7k9ZQ_Y0tRlVpuZVr91c4ytdtNLGjcVHNU-O-K7OgkofoEA7zv-DDqQI-WnpiEzsG6UHK-rAZ1F48NSXj1okVAMmgeHjwFyTweb5jEl2sV1gDFJueqn8VXaLL_bnHgxBRlNDFxPf_NxnyKcfiAJCUJ920GfRZdAfYZPKBgxoxFblYytraGgVwhXd9E7aJXBGRDVj-SJWKY4WO5TJP1XzH6JTqiT1qb3a_OH5NH7PTys4j2CqMzIFZn)**

| [**DONATE NOW**](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=99a22f3f453f4c79a55626c1dc359e21&_e=jndkyAFdyAhVyo-8s3aWHd-9B2974FiaPf0R6CagLNZat-hEZueE0Csq7LH32HvBG6ZWnKuXUsy4WIgFz5dxL9086CU9GgfHkCGtPFYckpDP_TmslB0kao6vppedRF4hG6GGo7RsuW6qnzlarKXd5S6KchSPqZPUfZjCOsiaE6u1A-0_oiAzPzlmkoLQaXT3qrJSYGbQa2KGgEYggwL_0n1_FXdv_GoOT4OcKviT4BZtA2QU8VWA3Szy_BwA1ulP7jEQMp5BPUzvNtRjIVxXuRNYWyZTah-7xVpBj0HONk7B8gW5PPzLHul9_Gpt2uidQGDD3iuQL4bAyosjkciI4fyeDsalIMq3JNkrrqjfuPNIdPfCQuWdYp_HhL7lc-ipoEkBlqkmqV-DU-VbdIBdcgY_1YIIZ232zcJ3BOMyu5hCbktfg3h4W8pXQzFJjdVAHraBCX1PQrJL8LrJ-wiHNCvvOxKZTY4SSy_NDQxbJtKEXSVoChCGeWcxM7ztxBH7gAZDWovE9PURMJfOyWQQxejr-m_fezucisH2sj2zdvhCxAEQUrjvUcGgMSt2-fvh)

body:
Tonight is our deadline
Hi there - I hope you're doing well. Sorry for the weekend email, I hate bothering you, so I'll keep this short.
Tonight is the midpoint of the month, and Morghan just told me we are $1,523 behind our mid-June goal. Our FEC deadline is at the end of this month and we have to have a strong showing.
I've prepared a link for you here, if you're in a position to give before midnight tonight:
**[http://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109653074/551815442/-200729754?refcode=250615_np_mm2_l1&ms=250615_np_mm2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTU0MDYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGZkOTFmMmUtNGE0YS1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lTFLpEfaXIgfrH44SlTzVC2gsExEuwXZfxp_nZ361S0=)**
You should know that I go to work each day with only one thing in mind: serving New Jersey families as best I can. Nobody – no special interest and no attack ad — will get my eye off the ball.
Thank you for your support.
— Nellie

Texas could be the tipping point for the entire U.S. Senate - but only if we build the Democratic warchest right now. We can’t afford to wait. Rush $10 or more right now to help flip Texas and take back the Senate. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-flipasenateseat?refcode=050625_2]

Texas Republicans aren’t just picking a Senate nominee.
They’re deciding how deep they want to sink into extremism.
🔴John Cornyn wants to drag the party back to the Bush era.
🔴Ken Paxton wants to torch what’s left and build a MAGA empire on the ashes.
🔴And national Republicans are quietly cheering them on.
But here’s the real headline:
If we flip this seat, we flip the U.S. Senate.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dd7bd839-2a85-4044-85e5-2229e5078294?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/4600cc88-2185-4500-b038-2dd08a17bd50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/46d20499-19dd-4bf8-8047-5de228f3c5a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas ' biggest political donor is calling out elected officials after striking out in the Legislature,**" Houston Chronicle's**Dylan McGuinness, Neena Satija** and**Matt Zdun** - "Leaders for Texans for Lawsuit Reform, the [biggest donor](https://substack.com/redirect/bad997d0-980c-4f90-9517-36c515ca7b65?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Texas politics, say they have a simple strategy when trying to persuade state lawmakers: "We never make enemies," President **Lee Parsley** said in late April. "We only make friends."

But now that the Texas legislative session has concluded [without lawmakers passing](https://substack.com/redirect/7379225a-c34a-4fa7-a3db-f92a6828821e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) any of the group's three high priority bills, TLR is taking a decidedly different tack.

In a [blistering letter](https://substack.com/redirect/3f4f1bac-f94a-48e5-8d7f-c7b218aaceb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to members, Parsley called out by name the lawmakers he said stifled TLR's agenda and all but promised to take them on in primary campaigns next March. He laid much of the blame on House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** ' shoulders.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/24ce1c98-9615-4886-b0bf-a962537a4e48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The group's political action committee "must redouble our efforts to elect strong, ethical, legislators who value a civil justice system that has integrity," Parsley wrote in his letter to the group's members last week.

Its signature priority, [Senate Bill 30](https://substack.com/redirect/0614fa8c-da29-4522-9c59-26eb8d1ed34d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - an effort to rein in medical costs in personal injury lawsuits - died after the House and Senate passed vastly different versions of the bill and could not reconcile the differences.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/24ce1c98-9615-4886-b0bf-a962537a4e48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I think it's fair to say we may look at backing some primary challengers," Parsley told the Houston Chronicle. "We'll take a good look at what happened toward the end of session and decide how to engage politically, but the people who did not support TLR's bill fully are certainly people who will be a focus for us."

The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice [code in the 1990s and early 2000s](https://substack.com/redirect/5c710569-fb5d-4e02-813f-e12de871fe55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group - led by Houston's most prolific political donor, the homebuilder**Richard Weekley** - was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state's business community.

The group's political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group's wishes.

"It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn't, it feels like they're throwing a tantrum about it," said **Andrew Cates** , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. "Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice."

TLR's letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. **Marc LaHood** , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.**Mitch Little** , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill "ineffective." It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year's GOP primaries.

Cates said the group's criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows [has been endorsed](https://substack.com/redirect/bcc1763c-39cb-4efc-a54f-14a4398a112c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions.

"The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss," Cates said.

When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, "Not ready to comment on that."

Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. "Simply put, TLR lies," LaHood wrote in a response on X.

Little told the Chronicle, "Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I'll just say on my part, I forgive them and I'm not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed."

Burrows' office did not respond to requests for comment. But Little said TLR's claim that Burrows led the effort to tank the legislation is "not true in any way."" [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/4a0195e0-da99-4ca0-a19a-8b24e08afd40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
* * *

TOP NEWS "Texas ' biggest political donor is calling out elected officials after striking out in the Legislature," Houston Chronicle'sDylan McGuinness, Neena Satija andMatt Zdun - "Leaders for Texans for Lawsuit Reform, the in Texas politics, say they have a simple strategy when trying to persuade state lawmakers: "We never make enemies," President Lee Parsley said in late April. "We only make friends." But now that the Texas legislative session has concluded any of the group's three high priority bills, TLR is taking a decidedly different tack. In a to members, Parsley called out by name the lawmakers he said stifled TLR's agenda and all but promised to take them on in primary campaigns next March. He laid much of the blame on House Speaker Dustin Burrows ' shoulders. The group's political action committee "must redouble our efforts to elect strong, ethical, legislators who value a civil justice system that has integrity," Parsley wrote in his letter to the group's members last week. Its signature priority, - an effort to rein in medical costs in personal injury lawsuits - died after the House and Senate passed vastly different versions of the bill and could not reconcile the differences. "I think it's fair to say we may look at backing some primary challengers," Parsley told the Houston Chronicle. "We'll take a good look at what happened toward the end of session and decide how to engage politically, but the people who did not support TLR's bill fully are certainly people who will be a focus for us." The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice , spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group - led by Houston's most prolific political donor, the homebuilderRichard Weekley - was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state's business community. The group's political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group's wishes. "It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn't, it feels like they're throwing a tantrum about it," said Andrew Cates , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. "Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice." TLR's letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. Marc LaHood , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.Mitch Little , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill "ineffective." It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year's GOP primaries. Cates said the group's criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions. "The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss," Cates said. When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, "Not ready to comment on that." Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. "Simply put, TLR lies," LaHood wrote in a response on X. Little told the Chronicle, "Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I'll just say on my part, I forgive them and I'm not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed." Burrows' office did not respond to requests for comment. But Little said TLR's claim that Burrows led the effort to tank the legislation is "not true in any way."" * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas ' biggest political donor is calling out elected officials after striking out in the Legislature,**" Houston Chronicle's**Dylan McGuinness, Neena Satija** and**Matt Zdun** - "Leaders for Texans for Lawsuit Reform, the [biggest donor](https://substack.com/redirect/6e76e64c-166e-46d7-bd76-db228350aa0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Texas politics, say they have a simple strategy when trying to persuade state lawmakers: "We never make enemies," President **Lee Parsley** said in late April. "We only make friends."

But now that the Texas legislative session has concluded [without lawmakers passing](https://substack.com/redirect/cdf442eb-1bf6-482a-8783-b007ea894037?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) any of the group's three high priority bills, TLR is taking a decidedly different tack.

In a [blistering letter](https://substack.com/redirect/c866f467-ffc2-47e9-bd8b-075ad2de2f4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to members, Parsley called out by name the lawmakers he said stifled TLR's agenda and all but promised to take them on in primary campaigns next March. He laid much of the blame on House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** ' shoulders.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/df9d1c4a-30f6-4bb0-b164-9116c608863a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The group's political action committee "must redouble our efforts to elect strong, ethical, legislators who value a civil justice system that has integrity," Parsley wrote in his letter to the group's members last week.

Its signature priority, [Senate Bill 30](https://substack.com/redirect/e1e76dfc-b477-48e9-978d-9fe9b6c4b5b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - an effort to rein in medical costs in personal injury lawsuits - died after the House and Senate passed vastly different versions of the bill and could not reconcile the differences.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/df9d1c4a-30f6-4bb0-b164-9116c608863a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I think it's fair to say we may look at backing some primary challengers," Parsley told the Houston Chronicle. "We'll take a good look at what happened toward the end of session and decide how to engage politically, but the people who did not support TLR's bill fully are certainly people who will be a focus for us."

The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice [code in the 1990s and early 2000s](https://substack.com/redirect/a64f5765-5e5b-413b-88f7-1bfd6319699d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group - led by Houston's most prolific political donor, the homebuilder**Richard Weekley** - was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state's business community.

The group's political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group's wishes.

"It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn't, it feels like they're throwing a tantrum about it," said **Andrew Cates** , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. "Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice."

TLR's letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. **Marc LaHood** , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.**Mitch Little** , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill "ineffective." It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year's GOP primaries.

Cates said the group's criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows [has been endorsed](https://substack.com/redirect/96c145f1-7c04-4f4a-a78d-bce7bb7d7b35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions.

"The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss," Cates said.

When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, "Not ready to comment on that."

Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. "Simply put, TLR lies," LaHood wrote in a response on X.

Little told the Chronicle, "Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I'll just say on my part, I forgive them and I'm not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed."

Burrows' office did not respond to requests for comment. But Little said TLR's claim that Burrows led the effort to tank the legislation is "not true in any way."" TOP NEWS "Texas ' biggest political donor is calling out elected officials after striking out in the Legislature," Houston Chronicle'sDylan McGuinness, Neena Satija andMatt Zdun - "Leaders for Texans for Lawsuit Reform, the in Texas politics, say they have a simple strategy when trying to persuade state lawmakers: "We never make enemies," President Lee Parsley said in late April. "We only make friends." But now that the Texas legislative session has concluded any of the group's three high priority bills, TLR is taking a decidedly different tack. In a to members, Parsley called out by name the lawmakers he said stifled TLR's agenda and all but promised to take them on in primary campaigns next March. He laid much of the blame on House Speaker Dustin Burrows ' shoulders. The group's political action committee "must redouble our efforts to elect strong, ethical, legislators who value a civil justice system that has integrity," Parsley wrote in his letter to the group's members last week. Its signature priority, - an effort to rein in medical costs in personal injury lawsuits - died after the House and Senate passed vastly different versions of the bill and could not reconcile the differences. "I think it's fair to say we may look at backing some primary challengers," Parsley told the Houston Chronicle. "We'll take a good look at what happened toward the end of session and decide how to engage politically, but the people who did not support TLR's bill fully are certainly people who will be a focus for us." The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice , spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group - led by Houston's most prolific political donor, the homebuilderRichard Weekley - was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state's business community. The group's political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group's wishes. "It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn't, it feels like they're throwing a tantrum about it," said Andrew Cates , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. "Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice." TLR's letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. Marc LaHood , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.Mitch Little , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill "ineffective." It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year's GOP primaries. Cates said the group's criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions. "The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss," Cates said. When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, "Not ready to comment on that." Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. "Simply put, TLR lies," LaHood wrote in a response on X. Little told the Chronicle, "Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I'll just say on my part, I forgive them and I'm not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed." Burrows' office did not respond to requests for comment. But Little said TLR's claim that Burrows led the effort to tank the legislation is "not true in any way."" The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice [code in the 1990s and early 2000s](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eHGrAk6NV4PC6YNW3buc6RLBFccPjAup09qelshuNbZik1dZbfJeau5qYufHMrJPzWvcsGLQixDxmnxU2XFOhiFdR0Ae2qQd7vewmgGCYqnxu7-Sh4mI-9d4iv7gFiNUghcNktyhvqqzxxBHnKv-r_wVmWXOlxi0_vujZgAI_M3IQQ1TAvygKxV8Rko8ZotaE7vrT5xo0skQ40knV05F5H_mWgR1-Jr3GW2rl6KbeE53R0kZa-ergU3yw45Eldey1bnO9Qcc2PotHUJ0fbNBarmf4xxNcb8l0s8i38-qIKK7tH2iJ8o3EZkVFnUg0xrenuh3JbxGpvlbElMKtmY6Bygym-mV9tsmGjuDejSGvqOARYd9Kc_i3bNGp5kgu0FU4WtJ-xc6k_scQjglp9PeibCQGgqhBBp7LjGzi_aQFqqoaEWtREYAq8fE03K8RxDpGXkrWDXfxqCc_NTyGC0baBDRt5hGQ6sMNaK6o3Rp6LMh/4he/kwYLM78XRQKzWa9WKiz2-g/h16/h001.Ga2FEHI-DZGArgiEzN08CsHVvZtq4Bn7xvgGVn2EtEo), spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group – led by Houston’s most prolific political donor, the homebuilder**Richard Weekley** – was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state’s business community.

The group’s political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group’s wishes.

“It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn’t, it feels like they’re throwing a tantrum about it,” said **Andrew Cates** , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. “Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice.”

TLR’s letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. **Marc LaHood** , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.**Mitch Little** , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill “ineffective.” It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year’s GOP primaries.

Cates said the group’s criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows [has been endorsed](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.5lwqIBNPgUpXckHvDM7-mB_EgAFulgFYS0mKGovREGLeLmZ-zmbzQQ_1x6WiFbc97EjwqyN-J4gLcKcr4GAdmGEKBhlcTZEa4RTLn-aRF_nSKCX-4DOIig7YNY_gNtq4MP6K9Oax3kY_f_iwCqLrwauMjVtM2NbvnV-8wUwflzX0KbHacYetZuma79J6pkYw_u0CrSfX6jS61BXH6XEAfR-ABSGJ89tuRnie0dUsYfpBxrNm83Jxmw1ZFNy8M6c-OwWtNNSc2ryZMWHLyvvilRM3meDcag5gLOcMxxoO91EcbCErxlR2FsQTrnzdvvD3NN8ToooD7P9P5aGz3Pf7XBcfXyGIRl6RmHCNaipW_KdQw8GLhoR1DaUq8w5uVouOs8mzIV9MiLjeCtxNO6L39MOutk6DzsPebJHadAqRHdsCOAmF03ngDedw9B7yPw_VNH7xsoMvlTWQXh6DzG9AIfaayNS-HO2qQ_XS1m-WTOUQeE_MVupD68KoDIGYf49d0JtGTKgrpzo_oEQebpsPUlgXesCoYfWwh-kjnXdOc0-48z9yXxBuWHu2FdTRhIqzQh1EQjLCqBruQmEHTa15OFNgEu4ShZdLq12eaogqbySccvRpwAcKfcnG3487zcWc1s0Y1cPENbmwNaPzbwDmYs-VChRlS2skEOk3C5FZd5E/4he/kwYLM78XRQKzWa9WKiz2-g/h17/h001.nbGz3xumgzM66OQRHRpLsgCyKozipnHNGU5cMKm2AvE) by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions.

“The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss,” Cates said.

When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, “Not ready to comment on that.”

Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. “Simply put, TLR lies,” LaHood wrote in a response on X.

Little told the Chronicle, “Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I’ll just say on my part, I forgive them and I’m not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed.”

Burrows’ office did not respond to requests for comment. Read the most recent FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr | TOP NEWS ““Texas’ biggest political donor is calling out elected officials after striking out in the Legislature, ” Houston Chronicle'sDylan McGuinness, Neena Satija andMatt Zdun – “Leaders for Texans for Lawsuit Reform, the in Texas politics, say they have a simple strategy when trying to persuade state lawmakers: “We never make enemies,” President Lee Parsley said in late April. “We only make friends.” But now that the Texas legislative session has concluded any of the group’s three high priority bills, TLR is taking a decidedly different tack. In a to members, Parsley called out by name the lawmakers he said stifled TLR’s agenda and all but promised to take them on in primary campaigns next March. He laid much of the blame on House Speaker Dustin Burrows ’ shoulders. » The group’s political action committee “must redouble our efforts to elect strong, ethical, legislators who value a civil justice system that has integrity,” Parsley wrote in his letter to the group’s members last week. Its signature priority, – an effort to rein in medical costs in personal injury lawsuits – died after the House and Senate passed vastly different versions of the bill and could not reconcile the differences. » “I think it’s fair to say we may look at backing some primary challengers,” Parsley told the Houston Chronicle. “We’ll take a good look at what happened toward the end of session and decide how to engage politically, but the people who did not support TLR’s bill fully are certainly people who will be a focus for us.” The legislative strikeout on these civil justice bills marks a low point for TLR, which won massive rewrites of the Texas civil justice , spending millions to elect like-minded lawmakers and lobby them to pass the legislation. At its height, the group – led by Houston’s most prolific political donor, the homebuilderRichard Weekley – was largely seen as synonymous with the Texas Republican Party, positioning itself as the political voice of the state’s business community. The group’s political action committee remains the top political spender in the state, spending $21.2 million on legislative races in 2024. The tone of its letter suggests the group could be on a warpath in the March primary elections. Instead of protecting incumbents, TLR could begin targeting members who bucked the group’s wishes. “It did feel a little strange because TLR has basically gotten everything they wanted for a long time now, and the one time it seems like they didn’t, it feels like they’re throwing a tantrum about it,” said Andrew Cates , a Democratic legislative lawyer and former lobbyist in Austin. “Everybody else would have been licking their wounds and hanging back and trying to make nice.” TLR’s letter alleged Burrows placed skeptical lawmakers on the key committees charged with shepherding SB30. It also called out state Rep. Marc LaHood , R-San Antonio, the main holdout on the House committee that forced significant revisions to the legislation; and state Rep.Mitch Little , R-Lewisville, who helped win passage of an amendment that TLR said made the bill “ineffective.” It named more than a dozen other Republican members as well, several of whom defeated TLR-backed candidates in last year’s GOP primaries. Cates said the group’s criticism of Burrows was notable, since lobbying groups rarely take those kinds of disputes public. Burrows by President Donald Trump for another term, and speakers have broad power to block legislation in future sessions. “The political capital is going to be really wasted if you come at him and miss,” Cates said. When asked if TLR would support a primary candidate against the speaker, Parsley paused and said, “Not ready to comment on that.” Other lawmakers responded to the accusations with barbs of their own. “Simply put, TLR lies,” LaHood wrote in a response on X. Little told the Chronicle, “Obviously, they were upset with the outcome and looking for people to blame or attack, but I’ll just say on my part, I forgive them and I’m not offended by any of it. I understand that their policy agenda failed.” Burrows’ office did not respond to requests for comment. But Little said TLR’s claim that Burrows led the effort to tank the legislation is “not true in any way.”" ($) | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 6/15/2025
body:
Fathers, Flags and Freedom in the Face of Fury
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1sc0c2e1f3)
---
**June 15, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
What a weekend for America—and for Colorado.
We marked **Flag Day** , **President Trump’s birthday** , and **Father’s Day** , while thousands flooded the streets for the “No Kings” protests. In cities across our state, demonstrators marched to protest President Trump and the return of law and order. In some places, American flags were flown upside down—while just hours earlier in Washington, D.C., a military parade celebrated **[250 years of the U.S. Army](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/schb8a26e62)** with thunderous flyovers, patriotic music, and cheers from crowds who still love this country.
That’s the story of our nation right now: two very different visions for America.
As Nina Anderson wrote in her [Flag Day reflection](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s8m7266b986), “The flag still stands for something real—something worth defending.” And I agree.
While some tore down, others lifted up. We celebrated fathers—the men who raised us, taught us grit, and carried us through. Pastor Drake reminded us in his powerful piece that fatherhood is [“the frontline of faith and family.”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbjcc545174) We need more of that.
And while the Left protests, the Right is building. We’re just five days away from our biggest celebration yet: **[the Mountain Majesty Gala on Friday, June 21st!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5qa701dfd3)**
We’re honored to welcome [Eric Trump as our keynote speaker](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4s798749e7) for this unforgettable night of patriotism, purpose, and power.
🙏 A huge thank you to our sponsors who are making this event possible: **[Adam Yormack](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/scie39cbb4c), [CLE Cigar Company](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s0o48fde81f), [Exectras](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/samf0449773), [10th Mountain Whiskey](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/ser00f89c56)**, Frieda Wallison, [ACC](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3nf22a57bd), [Smoker Friendly](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sekaf432429), [Elbert Appraisal Services](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5l8ff89ffe), [Lipstick Tactical](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5rcc0f94a4), [XX-XY Athletics](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbm48b5a64f), [Colorado Right to Life (CRTL)](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s7l790c6333), [Gregory Carlson](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s6p04bdf908), [Truth and Liberty](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/scmd33d109e), [Telephone Town Hall Meeting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s0rdab4875c), [Purple Label](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4ra49a79f3), and [Arena Mail & Digital](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sev07ce303b).
**🖥️ Pardon our dust—RMV has a new look!**
_We just launched our newly redesigned website to better serve our readers and showcase our growing team and content—but we’re still ironing out a few kinks. If things load slowly or look a little off, don’t worry—we’re on it! Thanks for your patience as we level up Rocky Mountain Voice for the next chapter._ _Check it out at[rockymountainvoice.com](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3g70443182) and let us know what you think!_
Let’s make Colorado proud. Let’s show the country what it looks like when patriots rise—not with riots, but with resolve.
In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[DOJ requests Colorado’s 2020 and 2024 voter data—Griswold’s ‘Gold Standard’ gets tested](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1uc24396df)** [FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3ie3ffc2cf) Colorado’s Secretary of State revealed Wednesday that the U.S. Department of Justice asked Colorado to turn over all records that relate to the 2024 federal elections and preserve all records from the 2020 election.
**[Webb: A ‘yes’ vote for Home Rule is a vote for Douglas County’s future](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/seu5bb040ee)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/seie368255f)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1k6eab450b) _On June 24, 2025, Douglas County residents will have the opportunity to vote in a Special Election to decide whether to form a Home Rule Charter Commission and elect 21 commission members to draft a potential Home Rule Charter._

**[False claims, real consequences: Judge rejects activist’s First Amendment defense](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s7vbfcc99a4)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4je0057fe4)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4s7ea630c4) For more than a year, Derrick Wilburn – father, community leader and now an elected school board member – was publicly branded a “child predator” by a vocal district parent. The accusations, repeated at school board meetings, online and even in state legislative testimony, painted him as a man who preyed on children.
|
---
**STATE**
**[From real estate to crypto to Colorado: Eric Trump to speak at RMV Mountain Majesty Gala](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sav9b6cc6b2)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sei65e3a01d)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s7j5f14a5c1) They didn’t want this event to happen. Activists have spent weeks pressuring the venue, trying to intimidate organizers into pulling the plug. But the Rocky Mountain Voice’s first Mountain Majesty Gala is moving forward – and Eric Trump is headlining it.

**[Priced out, packed in: Colorado renters squeezed by costs, policy gridlock and population growth](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s8l43a5a231)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/scma4fe5271)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4jd6acd7d3) Colorado’s affordable housing crunch – especially in the rental sector – has been building for years. Reports from the Common Sense Institute and Bell Policy Center trace the tipping point to around 2015, when demand consistently began outpacing supply.

**[Colorado Senate Republicans elect leadership team focused on families, farms and affordability](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1ie78af66c)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3g4fd450eb)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s6r02e9f727) As the Colorado State Senate prepares to welcome new leadership, the upper chamber’s storied traditions—from appointing its first chaplain in 1885 to its streamlined legislative process—set the stage for a fresh chapter in state governance.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s6tb7b85a89)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[D.C. parade marks 250 years of U.S. Army history with crowd, color and chopper thunder](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4m66c25294)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s4t8914fc21) A man in colonial uniform rode as George Washington on horseback during the Army’s 250th birthday military parade. It was the kickoff to one of the biggest military parades Washington has seen in decades.

**[Israel launches precision military strike targeting Iran’s nuclear program](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5v157cc5a2)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5of7c92290)**[WASHINGTON EXAMINER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sdnc171cf64) Israel conducted a military operation targeting Iran’s nuclear program on Thursday, hours after President Donald Trump cautioned Israel against one.

**[The protest-to-riot pipeline: How leftist networks mobilized and funded LA unrest](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s8i55f02f10)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s9o680e788e)**[DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1s9f606a9d) Well-funded activists and a coordinated social media campaign helped spark the pro-illegal migrant protests on Friday that descended into days of rioting in Los Angeles.
**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3ibbd0682f)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Released and reloaded: Montrose bond records show cracks feeding Colorado’s criminal underworld](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5iadd530c2)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s3jfb54cd61)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s8mbae09d2f) When a Montrose County woman was released on a $0 personal recognizance bond after skipping court in late 2023, it wasn’t her first time facing charges. It wouldn’t be her last either. Within nine months, she was arrested again – this time for second degree assault, harassment and criminal attempt. Her story is not an outlier.

**[The Mile High price tag: Denver now among worst in U.S. for inflation](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sag61e9dc8e)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s0o4055f8ca)**[WESTWORD](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s0k7b65ad08) Denver has a highly ranked park system and was recently named one of the coolest cities in the country, but living around the Mile High City isn’t cheap. According to a new study by WalletHub, the Denver metro has the fifth-worst inflation problem in the United States, with costs rising on a monthly and yearly basis.

**[D-11 school board votes to adopt policy requiring sports participation based on biological sex](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/scp74ff4320)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/scn3c81f8b8)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s1i95c23a09) During a special meeting on Wednesday, Colorado Springs’ District 11 became the second El Paso County school district to approve a new policy requiring students to participate in school sports based on their biological sex.

**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s8t4ac6648c)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Parker: We need a national, armed citizen militia now](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s2o209167ec)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbv93312190)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/seu947b11ae) _I am not surprised at the result of the efforts of people in our state to banish firearms. No one should be surprised at the open contempt for our God given rights, protected in our Bill of Rights, that was demonstrated by requiring Ken DeGraaf to cover part of our Constitution._
__
**[Garbo: Sanctuary policies led to chaos—and now they’re blaming ICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbnaf8f5a56)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s9q1d28fb07)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5lf863914b) _The chaos unfolding in Los Angeles isn’t just disturbing, it’s the natural result of years of political cowardice and ideological extremism. The riots erupting in response to ICE’s lawful enforcement of deportation orders are not acts of civil disobedience. They are acts of defiance against the rule of law itself._
__
**[Hunter: More than a price tag—what that doggie in the window really cost](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sap0fd6ded2)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5n1b602263)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5ledd60123) _How much is that doggie in the window? Remember this cheerful tune? It’s a jingle from another era, when the dream of a family pet felt as simple as a walk past a shop window. But today, that question echoes with heavier meaning._ __
**[Devotional: The belt of truth—where trust finds its anchor](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5gbe33890a)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbjcfe4a169)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/sbp82d9018a) _If there’s one thing that holds relationships, communities, and even entire cultures together, it’s trust. But trust isn’t something that floats around waiting to be found—it’s something built. And like any lasting structure, it’s built on a solid foundation._
__
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.-CFQ/s5s48a2c42b)**

**🌈 DID YOU KNOW?** Thanks to cone cells in the retina, your eyes can distinguish about **10 million shades** —an incredible feat of biology. These light-sensitive cells send signals to your brain, helping you perceive color in vivid detail. And for a rare few with a condition called tetrachromacy? They may see hundreds of millions of colors the rest of us can’t even imagine.

clean_body: Fathers, Flags and Freedom in the Face of Fury | | | | June 15, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear Readers, What a weekend for America—and for Colorado. We marked Flag Day , President Trump’s birthday , and Father’s Day , while thousands flooded the streets for the “No Kings” protests. In cities across our state, demonstrators marched to protest President Trump and the return of law and order. In some places, American flags were flown upside down—while just hours earlier in Washington, D.C., a military parade celebrated with thunderous flyovers, patriotic music, and cheers from crowds who still love this country. That’s the story of our nation right now: two very different visions for America. As Nina Anderson wrote in her , “The flag still stands for something real—something worth defending.” And I agree. While some tore down, others lifted up. We celebrated fathers—the men who raised us, taught us grit, and carried us through. Pastor Drake reminded us in his powerful piece that fatherhood is We need more of that. And while the Left protests, the Right is building. We’re just five days away from our biggest celebration yet: We’re honored to welcome for this unforgettable night of patriotism, purpose, and power. 🙏 A huge thank you to our sponsors who are making this event possible: , , , , Frieda Wallison, , , , , , , , , , , and . 🖥️ Pardon our dust—RMV has a new look! We just launched our newly redesigned website to better serve our readers and showcase our growing team and content—but we’re still ironing out a few kinks. If things load slowly or look a little off, don’t worry—we’re on it! Thanks for your patience as we level up Rocky Mountain Voice for the next chapter. Check it out at and let us know what you think! name: The Flyover
subject: Minnesota Shooter Arrested, Dollar Princesses, and Rabbit vs. Duck
body:
A suspect accused of killing Minnesota House Speaker Melissa Hortman and wounding State Senator John Hoffman and his wife was arrested on Sunday evening following the largest manhunt in state history. Vance Luther Boelter, 57, was found armed and hiding near Green Isle after authorities located his car and belongings.

Today we're featuring a [classic optical illusion](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGvnDAQhH-N6YyMMeZSuEhDd2lTImMvxybGS-w90P376CLlFa997adPM6P5eZ_vHhPmxw-_g-OV5gxXTcAMZd4xZ6jEvm5EDMWXApUhxlkrPUhlZWfn6DG9ZtNEtwJo6Btw3TgoZW9GDc3mrOpVGO1glhCMusVRrcGv2ujRGwvRNujeacp2tlPKmr79doN-XI0J0C0mLIMwCiIylfYXYeYN1vSiE0obaG-S25iPKvrvQk9CT9d1taGAZzxhSfTnLQk9_UcScz2weEbKQk90MAafJKb0rEi5Cj2hzHBCkb8zXJI3rBIDZQzysywZUqryRU-50SU_Kt7AF2iKi8eFKWG1ozDqsXtM_zbzl44-nf4bAAD__16pl_o) that claims if you see a duck first, you're creative--if it's a rabbit, you're analytical--so tell us: did your result match how you see yourself? ## Trump Blocks Israeli Proposal to Strike Iran Leader
President Trump issued a [stern warning to Iran on Sunday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OpDAQhU9jMlrlwhg6cLCJs9l0Q-TGxVArY7fsmkHcfsVeYbIveHp_fz6Wj8CJ8-fvcJCTrSyZzpZIhOpycM7UioS2lyJUQ63UhGJcEHDswfbaLjFwuhbTRbcRIQ0dOT2NAHY2MHa7ew7zCjOiptGsMI0Qgn6u0eiNXpPWU8fudgOrrQawZng8ZxqmzZiV9Musr1EZoMhS6uNv4Sw7bekq31Qfazm65HaRd1PDL4VeoT_P8_HVgpQYrlug0Dcp9VLo72UK_bskFl5vvIMVerAKvb5J6tfx7mWvFIRy67mG3FeSkDgIl9xzq4GSQj8b1NPTWIBJoe-qi--TU-JmJ2Xg8wic_veTH5367fBfAAAA___aPomj), stating that any attack on U.S. interests would be met with an unprecedented military response. He emphasized that the United States had no involvement in Israel's recent strikes on Iranian territory. Trump also vetoed an Israeli [proposal to assassinate Iran's Supreme Leader](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ8GOiL_DDYULrZJl223RGN7CN41GNmzifL2V9xXuOXRkc7R9_15LA9MOR3P37iT47UsB71bJmaqy56Og1phbFspTBVrpcYU46KEGgdhBmmWiCl_FuijW4kU6Z6ctKMQZgIx9pvTyiB4siQBYJ7i7HHFSUqtvRhnkn1y15ow0kghDOjbPJG2K0Ag6SH4sQNBMXGpt78lHbzRmj_lRfUWyt5ntzGfrdO_OnXv1B3PC-CqrlA5hUydunP9v59DqngMqVWkPCAzhn8DaNJeBgIbpQJrkWDUOswg9WS8Hfvq4vlOOadmbAfiuWPK38_8I10vp74CAAD__8U9fQw) Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a move U.S. officials feared could dangerously escalate the conflict. The ongoing hostilities have [resulted in casualties](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OqzAQxfGnMV3QMP4gFC5uQ5fbbolsPA7eGJw1o7B5-xX7Clsf6Sed_8dturmU03b_71ayHMu00bFnYqY6rWnbaC_s9qUUpupqpZ0phAkB9QXMpTNTcCm_J9UEG4mQZEO26zWAuSrQzWLRed3LQQ7KSAJp4oA-Dj1FH6RW_bVJ9tTAdKYDMEq2w5VkH5WaqfNq9loooJC41PazpI0XivldXlTbuaxNtgvzcxfyn8BR4HgcR-v9fG4Cx_OMwDGnFwkcZ3x8PQLI701zU214HinntJteKLivLuVfkf-U4WXxJwAA__-c3HC6), with Israeli airstrikes reportedly killing at least 406 people in Iran, while Iranian missile attacks have killed at least 13 people in Israel. Despite the escalating violence, Trump expressed [optimism about brokering a peace deal](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u3CAURleDOyz-jGcKikiRuxelS2lhuNg3wjCCOx7N7qPJFl79SZ_OOX--1i-PGcv-y5_gKNW1wKtnIIK2nlgK9Eq-H7USNN8adIIYVyXUxIXl0q7RY36vZoguASjQAzg5T0LYmxHTcLhNRysMxARiFkZK7e_JQ_Dgk1J3bwZ0nzdhpZVCWKPH-w30nIwJIDcTtokZARGptvFvxUIHpPyuF7Qx1HPI7iB6dKZ_MLUwtfgtfAzGvX5mppbfNSNh6Ewt1J7ngz8734E6x3LVfEHk2JuHzLH5wkMtKWMgppZOtb2ZXjAy_VMqdbPTZOXQXHy8MGfsdmZG7KfH_J-EvpXvcupfAAAA__-90oVl) between Israel and Iran, asserting that such an agreement is achievable.
## Trump Media Activates $2.3B Bitcoin Plan
Trump Media's [$2.3 billion crypto initiative launched](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3u2yAUBfCnwRsRBkySgSFV5f-UqlKHjhaGS3IrPiy4iZW3r9xX6HqOdHT0-31f7g4TlscPl8FSrEuBvScggrZkLAV6JdeftRI01xp0ghAWKeTEheGjWYLD9Fn0EGwEkKAGsON5EsJctJiGpzWTk6O4Knc5i1F574yazFU4iFFpvcoB7bEmzGhGIYxWp-sF1Dlq7WFctV8npgUEpNpOfyoWekJMn_qGdvI1D8k-ibbO1I3Jmcl53_dT7uXomJyh8Fdncs61wIfJ2de8uYJwZNReeeMZArrOGzywU3OEtfBYG1-RfMXCqYHrr_bhAVziK_iaoXOIETzhG5icXeO32_j1tf7cmJqrx8DU928HKez9F7RtaDZsO6aE3ZyZFo_sMP07T_8l_rbybwAAAP__lxiYGg) after the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) approved its registration statement, unlocking the resale of over 85 million shares tied to earlier financing deals. The company plans to use the funds to buy and hold Bitcoin as part of its corporate assets. This move aligns with a [broader trend of companies](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO4yAQhJ_G7ogAw9opKK5Jl2uvtNawjjlhsGCTyG9_8r3CX45GGs33_XnOT4wp5tdv3MnxWuZM35aImeq8x5ypFca2lcJUsVZqTCHMWmorJAgFc8CYztn0wa1EmoaenBqtlDAZafvNWYMABuQCkoagPSiPVnnyFnA1WvbRXWsSFCgpwQy3-0TDuBrjSS3GL7YzkkLkUm9_S8y80ZrO8qF682Xvk9uYj9YNvzr96PQDjwvgqq5QOfpEnX4skX2JWXAlbO96Cl_2A3OkJkLJmILg-t4PsUe_ISXR8EylCo0jjMtd-6B8mAY5TEsIMFpvaNUQoK8uHN-YUmwwdka-dozp_y_-kcyP0_8CAAD__4hgids), from Trump Media to Japanese hotel firm Metaplanet, adopting Bitcoin as a corporate asset. CEO Devin Nunes said the move supports growth across Trump Media's social, streaming, and financial platforms and aligns with its vision for the "Patriot Economy," which promotes financial independence and alternatives to mainstream institutions. A new brand, [Truth.Fi](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzz1uhDAQQOHT4G6RxxgbChdp6DZVpJSWf4ZlIoNXZrKrvX2UXCEHeE_6Pq_-GqjQcXsPOzpeqz_weRZkxuZ3Og48K4dzq5WxhdbwZMzZK6nGizQXMD4HKi-vRXYrosJBoAM7SmkmLUexuYgxrWOYYFLTrAwka4cIAHaGpHI0gtzvTRowIKXRQz9PONhV64QQdYpjpyVm4tr6r0oHb7iWV31g61PdRXEb870b3jq1dGr5aN-89QuJ5vL9SaXQaWyn5W0PVP4C_pfy4dRPAAAA__8jG2W1), is expected to offer [crypto-based services](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D0SmzAQxfHTQLeMEELYhYo0dE6bkhHSAuvRByNtIL59xrlCLvCb9_6_XsvLUqC0_7QRDW95SXjXgMxYlkgpYc1s65EzY7GlYGX0fpFCjiA09HrxlsJnUa03G6LEoUXTT6MQ-qHE2B7GSTUJpx5S-E09tX5OQjmPyqPTdsK-JfPVhO51L4RWQ_d84DBtSjnsV-XWsVECPXEu3TtT4gO38MkXls7l2AZzMJ-1GX40cm7kfN9358rn5JwovW3tEnIj5--rRs5cfscTInqyFSQMK6zELlOCmC-EPWOFQBeC3RgLVHSwF8QUaD8Y7sNyBZcjpR0S_vnCbTH-vCkEqnpqlNijpfBvGf9XzsvIvwEAAP__jJ2MvQ) and investment tools.
## American Women Who Married into Aristocracy
Between 1870 and 1914, more than [100 wealthy American women married into](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDtu6zAURFcjdRT4l12weI06vzalwM-lyYQSDfLKgncfOFlCugEGmDk4H7f1ZnPJ-_2_3cBgrOsOZy-ACG3d8r5Dr2h7qhWh2dagI4SwcsoVoZowvQaby2uVYzARgIMYwbBZUaovkqoxGSWtCNFehWI2xqCFFM7OAOrCQ2BOj9m816hmmlGqpZiuFxBzlNIDc9I7NUgKIWNt02fNOyaI5VWf0CZft7GYhPjog_g38GXgy3mek3P-3Q188UfBo8HAF9sw-_JOv2_0SlI9Sail2EYeLe8eeodOXKvHPSE5OonF5kawEkxAjq-xmfA4cym563mQ9L7ZXH4g8E_mnoZ_BwAA___Y34QG) Britain's cash-strapped aristocracy, bringing new money--and new attitudes--to old titles. Consuelo Vanderbilt, one of the most iconic _dollar princesses_ , was pushed into marrying the Duke of Marlborough and cried through her wedding, later calling life at Blenheim Palace deeply unhappy. [Watch a documentary about her](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1u8yAUxvGrgS3W4cPYHhhe6W22tEuljoiYQ0yFTQQnsXL3lXsLXZ9H-g3_r4u7-JTTdnv3K1qKxW24t4xEWN2atg1bId-WUgirrxUbYQhOguxPYE7CuOBTfjnNg42IEhVHK4YewIwaer5YYcSgVJAQJi-U7CcRwcCo4hiiGfzMkz00MMIIAKNVN42ohqj1jOKq52vPNGBIVGr3XdJGC8b8Kk-s3VxWnu1CdG9M_WPyzOR53_fuVR70uOLxH4uneWHq_GTqv_7AyaXT5-PtxqsN9z3lnJoZmIbb6lP-JelPHZ5W_gQAAP__1MNwzg). Nancy Astor, the sharp-tongued daughter of a Virginian railway tycoon, made history as the [first woman to sit in Parliament](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGO6yAUheHV4A4LMGCnoHiNu7x2SgvDJb4jDBFcxcruR5ktTHPK70j_1327e8xYHv_9CY5S3QpcPQMRtO3EUqBX8v2olaD51qATxLgpoQwXlku7RY_5vekhugSgYBrAydkIYRctzHC4_SbnkKQxi5jiIs2820VNkwxJ7iLMYUD30YSVVgph9TTeFpjmpHUAueuwG6YFRKTaxu-KhQ5I-V1f0MZQzyG7g-jZ2fSPqZWp9Sqh4Vjbg6n14zK1is8oy9RafAlv7jvVxukAnrB14lc9feFY-N6QsB9MrUNz8XlhztjtzLR4nB7z7x_9KdLLqZ8AAAD__4FJeeg), where she famously clashed with Winston Churchill. [Daisy Leiter of Chicago](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGunDAUBb8GOiNjjNktXKTZ7qVNiYx9WG5i7JXvzSP799HLL6QeaTSaHx_rR6BM5fk9nPCy17Xg4gwRtPWkUsBVAh-1ClpoDSxIaTXazEo7Nbo1Bcrv1fbJ74DB1MOPy6y1u1k994dP95Ruizbz3WwW4-buKWKflzHYZbpp9OS_bNqNbtTa2Wm43zAtu7UR42bjNndWI5HUNvysVOTAnt_1E22I9eyzP0Re3E3fOvPozOO6ruECy0mFBQ1_pIUh1uH3r848QhOKGZ15RBRhFUpSjMK0USZ5K5baCKw2HPSFqDzRWKWac2jq1ahEMIP75tPropyJ3dJZ_TwD5X858l8PP735GwAA__-BI4p_), a glamorous heiress with a mind of her own, found a rare love match with the Earl of Suffolk and even became a helicopter pilot in her later years. Cora Colgate, a Southern belle and toothpaste heiress, married Henry Byng, 4th Earl of Strafford, who died in a mysterious fall onto railway tracks just five months after their wedding. See [her gravestone here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu4yAYBk-Du1i_McZJQbGNu2y7JfoDHza7GCKMYmVP_5R3hdePZqT5c7d3jinm9TfvMC0Um3EeCa2h2j3mjKM0PrZSGirXiqPBeytJThfSl0FbzzG9req8CYDE2MEM80Skr4qmbjPuppUGk9OQ0hEoYFajc2qeaNZBdtF8bKQHPRBpNfa3K8Y5KOUwPJR7TEIRfGyl9n9LzG1DSO_yQu1d2btkttaehxh_CbkIuZzn2YeYPa-VX_ggQi479lIjJyEXSYOa1EyjkIsrlS1S_M8PtO3iSlq5wf5Dzlx9V41_njGleOhZKFp3jum72X406mXkVwAAAP__1ZV9fA).

Vance Luther Boelter, 57, was found armed and hiding near Green Isle after authorities located his car and belongings. | | | | | | Monday, June 16, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! body:

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | “We’re at the stage now where the inmates are not just running the asylum—they’re redesigning it.”— David Mamet, American playwright
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
# The Inmates Running The Asylum
# A No Kings Weekend Recap
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
# Vitriol Without a Cause: NoKing Protest Lacked Message, Overflowed with Hate

---
| Over the weekend, a brave brigade of octogenarian keyboard warriors took to the streets—armed with walkers, warbly speeches, and laminated signs—valiantly resisting the tyranny of **mean tweets.** Yes, the same folks who champion mandates for masks, mandates for cars, mandates for your kid’s classroom and your town’s zoning... are now warning us about kings.
**You can’t make this up. But don’t mistake their outrage for courage** —it’s **not protest, it’s projection.**
Be proud of where we’re headed. And frankly? I’ll take a mean tweet over this circus any day.

| | Hey Reader, I got you a gift for you... Something to help protect you and your family while you’re on the road. And you’re going to want this, especially if you travel with your CCW. Because you don’t want to go to court with nothing but a story... you need solid, indisputable evidence...
**[Which is why I’m giving away 500 of my new Dash Eagle cameras at below wholesale cost](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21577/6374453/3bddd95c6946e29f67af4e49711490d1f8205f66/90a2be998277991fe8bf072149c6aab0fb126e72)** Every time you hit the road, you’ll have an HD dashboard camera to record everything that happens on the road, and inside your vehicle... So if you get in an accident, an altercation, or if you get pulled over by the police... You’ll be able to **record every second** with crystal clear video and audio... And you’ll never have to worry about not having any proof to back your case in court. It installs in just seconds and you can charge it in your car while simultaneously recording... But like I said... [We’re only giving away 500 Dash Eagles for 80% OFF...](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21577/6374453/3bddd95c6946e29f67af4e49711490d1f8205f66/90a2be998277991fe8bf072149c6aab0fb126e72) And it’s first come, first served... subject: Guaranteed Income Nixed, Vape Shop Raids, and 'The Howling House'
body:
Harris County commissioners voted 3-1 Thursday to scrap a controversial guaranteed income program after more than a year of litigation.

| | |

# Monday, June 16, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1855, around 200 French colonists arrived at [La Réunion](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFyhCAUANDTYLfOB1l0C4o0dps2pfOFj_4Jwg6yq3v7THKFXOAV7-s-3ZEjp-UTN7L1DHlKdOyRaqUyLU8smCqR5-TyRolP8i980KRAXS9gLtJMHjm-J914O4O_yYas7K8AvTTm1qwWwtArPbsOceiMk52WhIMLpGWYIWDD9hcDI42UIEG14TY4LREdkkfoB6GBPNdc2rpSpRP3EN_5RaV1eWuiXWt97KL7EGoUajyOo637ijlFTtTmsgg1rpj8nPO3UCOlWph2ocaIl0LPxDk1xfrHwTHybnqhYdmQ459e_3fysuonAAD__0qId_I), a short-lived socialist community on the banks of the Trinity River in Dallas. Financial insolvency caused the society to collapse within 18 months. While thousands of people turned out for [protests across the state](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDFuhDAQRU8DnZE9iw0ULtLQbdqUaMADWDE2sifL7u2jzRVSf-k_vfd1n-7og4_bJx5k-bmmKdJVAjFTnrYfzBiZyPm4pIOif5J74EkTSNBCGqHM5NCH19TWzs7SDaomqzotZaeMGerdzr1W5jajGZSelZbG9KZbodeIGtYZa2_fZ9Ioo5RUEpp16JdWIS5IDmXXV60k5znlhndiemJZwys9KDdLOupgd-azVLePCsYKxuu6mhfuKb3XCsa3TgXjuSdORcQkvn3cijhzYipcBC45lSLg1ioN7QDNzkeos3Xn5UPwxXRVK7cDffjD8f8iPSz8BgAA__8f8HwS) this weekend, folks in Jacksonville and Nacogdoches were celebrating tomatoes and blueberries. Check out today's _Around Texas_ section for details. We're sponsored today by [Next Thing Technologies](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2PnDAQhn-N6UD2AN7bguKUDSmiW-WqRGnQYA_giz9WZthb_n1ElJPSpx09embmfb-_DC_ovIvzFQN1_JjSEOl99cRMeZg3zBiZyLpoUqDoHmTveKMBJLSl1KXSg0Xn96EpbDdKe1YFderUSnlSWp-LpRsbaG0DttFKwkijNvoMVkGL0E6NMYXrDpnUSisllYRqOj-ZRiEaJIvy9CQaSdZxyhUvxPTAdfJ7ulOuTAqF7xbm2yrqZwG9gD4yV5EezIuLc8VkFgH9xKuAvv70Of0Yqf3y7efXEt6u9XV_fX1u0Yu63zgMa9qyIVFfKKDz5eT3cvLHouM4aAXogwpk3RY-qL9Dg-GGbo6ivvyTX0YXA_6iPLxtkT7QFJkii_qCbkwpuDgXubO3d-e9W_VJNHI-zH--4__r5N7B7wAAAP__RhGj7w). ## County's Guaranteed Income Program Scrapped
Harris County commissioners voted 3-1 Thursday to [scrap a controversial guaranteed income program](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGPozAQBeBfYzrQeAA7LlxcQ5drr0QTewiWjI2ME5J_f8pusT9g6ye9p-_9u85XCjGk-1_a2NbXkufE5xG5Vi7z_UGFUmX2Ibm8cQov9k_aeUbAsQXVSjV7CvE9D423N_BGNmylHgG0VMo0q9XYG2VG7YFuI_deDctyM2iURO6dHptgP2WgpJISJGC3mIsbJJEj9gT6IgZgH2ouXV258ouOJb7zk0vn8tZEu9a6H6L_I3ASOJ3n2bm15PRJBU4fjsCJSg0ussBppVLC0br8SPXd_hDbb2OL0Gs1mEu3r3tTrN_PEGM4lBYD3DcK8Wu2_u6sp8X_AQAA__8jUn7-) after more than a year of litigation. Uplift Harris would have provided approximately 1,500 low-income families with [$500 a month for 18 months](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp_GdI7GE_xD4WIburvtlmiwh2Ctwcg4IfftV9lXuPUnfTrn_PmavijltD9-08a-vZcy7XydmVvjOj2eVGlvzDHtoWy8pzfHFx08IaCWYKQyU6SUv6e-i36GOKiOvbIawCpjhm71EYwDZiAb9NDrAFY7xB5pcbPTw9wl_zkDo4xSoABvy-BCr4gCcSSwTvTAMbVSb23lxm86l_xdXlxvoWxd9mtrxynuvwSOAsfrum5_1_L8jAJHqi2FzALHj5fAMZdAWeD4PHJamlyp1nTKo5ZHpU2e67PJWK5d4IhOy95oVos2Us0Esp8dyoFtkGDnIVg93-_q3lUfjyvlnE5jRQ-PjVL-z9Z-VvTl8V8AAAD__77XiGE) using federal COVID-19 relief funds. Attorney General Ken Paxton sued the county in April 2024 to block the program, arguing it was an unconstitutional allocation of public funds. The county tried to implement the program under a different name after the Texas Supreme Court ordered its suspension, prompting Paxton to sue again. An appeals court [ruled in Paxton’s favor](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb8Gb0SACY4Hhi7e0rWj9QLPNio2CJ6d5O-rpEM_oPOV7r3nfF3HK4QYtvkTVrT0mNK44b1GJMIyzjsU2AjRh82lFbfwQH9AxlEJdebCcGlGDyE-R914exO-lw1a2Z2F6KQxfbNYIyY_GdH1qDoxOWhb0L4H73VndCuxCfZVJow0Ugop1GnqL05LAAfoQXQXpgX6QKmcaEHCB9QpPtOB5eTS2kS7EOXK2g-mBqYGuDnZvhKmhpwqMTUsUEqo3KV9o2flG975Hxn_ReO5pLnAyvNOfEnRc5gIC88Fj5D2yt_D_BaT-2ZqkGdjtNSaqaEp1ud7iDFU0zEt5hVCfF-j_wk9rPoJAAD__6KDjE0) in December. Commissioners voted to [reallocate the funding](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L_upCAUxfGnwY4JoANaUGxjN9tuaa5wVRIEAtf58_ab2WSf4Fd_kpOT75_H8oAQQ9p_w4mW3lteEr5aRCKsy35BhUSIPiSXT0zhjf4JBRcl1J0LzaVePIT4WYbO21X4SXZopbkLYaTWU3dY1H7EXnk39tOo1tX0E2gBg5vQAG6-C_Y7JrTUUgop1G2bRjdIAAfoQZiRDQJ9oFxvdCDhG9oWP_mJ9eby2UV7EJXG-l9MzUzNsDrZf4WpueRGTM0H1Boad_lK9OEOksPY-FVi2IiXmvcKJy8RUuNXQy7Hk2c6sP63xtQstenvYlBMzV21vrxCjKFpwwaxnxDivy_0s4JPq_4GAAD__4VohoI) to other programs for those in need, including $8 million in rental assistance, $8 million toward homelessness diversion, and $2.5 million to financial assistance for food.

## Big Bend's Summer Death Rate Is Highest in U.S.
Big Bend National Park has the highest summer fatality rate among national parks, according to a recent study that examined data from 2007 to 2023. During that period, there were [1.55 deaths per 100,000 visitors](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OgzAQhJ_GdET2AiYULq6hy7VXoo13k1hnbGRv_t7-RF7h6tHM6Pt-TssJQwzp-o0rO3ld8pL4WSOLcFmudyyYhJlC8nnlFF5MD9x4AQ1Dq21r7EIY4nvpG3JnTZNp2Jlx0Ho01k7NzfXWn7kDYzpNzASA_jjYzowTDkSDbYLbx7Q11hhtNBwu09H3BtEjE-rxqHrNFCSXg9xY-IX1Et_5weXg89pEdxPZquq-FMwKZv_suj1QMO8kCuZPRcFMjBQDV2kTSsgJY7th-a1tva8rFwVzUxxtzxBjqHZUvb6uGOLnRf7n5uHgLwAA__8RMXli), with hyperthermia listed as the most common cause. Daytime temperatures at the park routinely soar over 100°F during the summer months. In June 2023, a Florida man and his 14-year-old son died while [hiking the difficult Marufo Vega Trail](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OpDAQBOCnMZlHbQMGAgeXkM2lF6IGt5nWmTYC7_y8_Yp9hc0qKH0l1b_7dEdOLOtf3MiXd8yT0OtMVAod0_qFB0ohCixL3kj4TeGJO00WbKvBaeOmgJw-U1MFP0MYTEXedC1AZ5wbqoevDcXoEFuwbWjRDX1tF4jRAMUmuqFif2HgjDMGDNhbHPqlMYgLUkDoetUABS75uJUHFXrjGdMnP-m4LXmrkn-Usp-q_qPsqOw48zqThJOksFC6OsqOFmyt7Ajuyr2yYz4SSsh6Q9EoQZ-F9jOLDkyaRc-86ovRgoWzYNI7Hv-VHavDh_3FKfHpOtXAuiH_jFTld_c9vf0OAAD__9hmhG0) in 119°F heat. The less popular Guadalupe Mountains National Park also made the list, with [two fatalities from motor vehicle crashes](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOtDaEDsULv4mXf72SrTYa1idsZFxQvL2J6J7g-s-zUjfSPN1H-_IgeP8H1cy5eXTGOnYA5VCeZwfmDEWIsfRppUiv8g9caNRgrw0oBqhRocc3mNfOzOBG0RNRugLgBZKDfViSEzkoLOexHUgD-S87GynB8RJC6SazSkDJZQQIEC2frjaXiBaJIegr1UP5Lik3JaFCr1w9-GdnpRbm9Y6mKWUba-6f5W8VfJ2HEdrl5zi2VbyFtjTGXNwv4i5sA0nTTw3E0XXbJi_m4-6cRhnyumxNxI63atOttuy1dm47eAQeFe66mFekcNnv_zttaeRPwEAAP__2cCC9g) during the years examined. Its summer fatality rate of 0.38 per 100,000 visitors ranked eighth among national parks. Park officials advise summer visitors to hike in the morning, wear appropriate clothing, carry plenty of water, and be aware of the symptoms related to heat exhaustion.

clean_body: Harris County commissioners voted 3-1 Thursday to scrap a controversial guaranteed income program after more than a year of litigation. | | | | | | Monday, June 16, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1855, around 200 French colonists arrived at , a short-lived socialist community on the banks of the Trinity River in Dallas. Financial insolvency caused the society to collapse within 18 months. While thousands of people turned out for this weekend, folks in Jacksonville and Nacogdoches were celebrating tomatoes and blueberries. Check out today's Around Texas section for details. We're sponsored today by . Be sure to check them out in today's edition. Tune in to The Flyover Podcast! New episodes drop daily—watch on YouTube or listen on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, all in under 15 minutes. | | County's Guaranteed Income Program Scrapped Harris County commissioners voted 3-1 Thursday to - after more than a year of litigation. Uplift Harris would have provided approximately 1,500 low-income families with using federal COVID-19 relief funds. Attorney General Ken Paxton sued the county in April 2024 to block the program, arguing it was an unconstitutional allocation of public funds. The county tried to implement the program under a different name after the Texas Supreme Court ordered its suspension, prompting Paxton to sue again. An appeals court in December. Commissioners voted to to other programs for those in need, including $8 million in rental assistance, $8 million toward homelessness diversion, and $2.5 million to financial assistance for food. Suspect Arrested in Capitol Threat While violence erupted at several “No Kings” protests across the U.S. on Saturday, demonstrations in Texas were relatively peaceful despite drawing crowds as large as 20,000 in , 15,000 in , and 11,000 in . State troopers arrested a person in La Grange suspected of toward lawmakers at the State Capitol on Saturday. An evacuation order for the building was lifted following the arrest. In sparsely populated Brewster County, a man was arrested during a protest after allegedly at a passing vehicle, injuring its driver. Gov. Greg Abbott had deployed and 2,000 Department of Public Safety troopers ahead of the planned protests, which coincided with a military parade in Washington, D.C., marking the U.S. Army’s 250th birthday. Big Bend's Summer Death Rate Is Highest in U.S. Big Bend National Park has the highest summer fatality rate among national parks, according to a recent study that examined data from 2007 to 2023. During that period, there were , with hyperthermia listed as the most common cause. Daytime temperatures at the park routinely soar over 100°F during the summer months. In June 2023, a Florida man and his 14-year-old son died while in 119°F heat. The less popular Guadalupe Mountains National Park also made the list, with during the years examined. Its summer fatality rate of 0.38 per 100,000 visitors ranked eighth among national parks. Park officials advise summer visitors to hike in the morning, wear appropriate clothing, carry plenty of water, and be aware of the symptoms related to heat exhaustion. | Flying together with our sponsor The Grid Can’t Handle AI — But This $6 Company Might AI is booming.
